pattern: A one-slot buffer between you and a consume-only stream lets you look at the upcoming element as often as you like: peek fills the slot from the stream, and next drains the slot before touching the stream again.
hints: The wrapped object can only move forward — once you call its next you cannot un-see the value, so store it somewhere.
Hold at most one element in a cache: peek fills the cache if empty and returns it without clearing it.
next returns and clears the cache when it is full, otherwise pulls straight from the wrapped iterator; hasNext is true when the cache is full or the wrapped iterator still has elements.

edge_inputs.description: peek before and after next on a three-element stream
single-element stream, peek repeatedly then consume
hasNext right after a peek that buffered the final element — must still be true
next-only usage — the buffer must not desync when peek is never called
alternating peek/next through the whole stream
duplicate values back to back — peek must return the buffered copy, not skip it

stress.description: random stream fully drained with random peek/hasNext probes between next calls
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['PeekingIterator']
    args = [[nums]]
    for _ in range(n):
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            ops.append('peek')
            args.append([])
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            ops.append('hasNext')
            args.append([])
        ops.append('next')
        args.append([])
    ops.append('hasNext')
    args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
